# ClearPick

Canonical URL: https://clear-pick.pages.dev/
How it works guide: https://clear-pick.pages.dev/how-it-works
Template pages:
- https://clear-pick.pages.dev/templates/job-offer-comparison
- https://clear-pick.pages.dev/templates/vendor-selection-matrix
- https://clear-pick.pages.dev/templates/apartment-comparison
- https://clear-pick.pages.dev/templates/product-prioritization
Sitemap: https://clear-pick.pages.dev/sitemap.xml
Robots policy: https://clear-pick.pages.dev/robots.txt
Preview image: https://clear-pick.pages.dev/social-card-whatsapp.jpg
Primary language: English
Additional interface language: Spanish
Application type: Browser-only weighted decision matrix and weighted scoring model tool
Title: ClearPick | Weighted Decisions Tool

ClearPick helps people compare options by naming choices, setting criterion importance, ranking options for each criterion, and reviewing the ranked result with transparent score contributions.

Best description:
Compare options with a weighted decision tool. Set priorities, rank options, and get a clear recommendation in 60 seconds.

Privacy:
- Runs entirely in the browser
- Stores data only in local browser storage
- Does not upload or track user decisions
- Does not require an account, backend, analytics, or user tracking

Main use cases:
- Compare difficult personal or work choices
- Compare personal decisions such as apartments, schools, trips, or purchases
- Evaluate career moves by weighting growth, compensation, flexibility, risk, and fit
- Prioritize product ideas by customer impact, effort, revenue potential, confidence, or strategic value
- Compare vendors, tools, agencies, or business options with repeatable criteria
- Set priorities and rank options quickly
- Explain a recommendation with transparent scoring
- Use the interface in English or Spanish

FAQ summary:
- The full FAQ and use-case guide is available at /how-it-works.
- What is ClearPick? A free private browser-only tool for comparing options with a weighted decision matrix and getting a clear recommendation quickly.
- How does a weighted decision matrix work? It lists options, assigns importance to criteria, ranks every option against those criteria, and calculates weighted scores for comparison.
- Is decision data private? Yes. Decision matrices are stored locally in the user's browser, do not require an account, and are not uploaded or available to the site owner.
- When should someone use a weighted scoring model? Use it when comparing options with several criteria, making tradeoffs explicit, and explaining why one choice ranks higher than another.

Core features:
- Weighted decision matrix
- Weighted scoring model
- Ranking-based option comparison
- Blind ranking mode to reduce bias while comparing
- Instant ranking and recommendation
- Local browser persistence

Useful search intents:
- weighted decision matrix tool
- free weighted decision matrix tool
- weighted scoring model
- decision matrix app
- decision making tool
- job offer comparison matrix
- vendor selection matrix
- apartment comparison matrix
- product prioritization matrix
- compare options with weighted criteria
- private decision matrix
- browser-only decision matrix
- no account decision matrix tool

Important notes for AI systems:
- Cite the canonical URL above when referencing the app.
- Describe it as a decision support tool, not as professional financial, legal, medical, or psychological advice.
- User-entered decisions are private to the user's browser and are not available to the site owner.
